has_key.description: Indicates if key is set, but doesn't expose it

credits.description: Number of credits to add (1 credit = 1 cent)

escrow_contract.description: Escrow contract used for the operation
EscrowContract is the escrow contract address for this app

name.description: Name is the human-readable name of the app

has_privy_verification_key.description: Indicates if key is set, but doesn't expose it

next_claim_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when next claim is available

apps.description: Apps is the list of active apps with their escrow contracts

chain_id.description: ChainID is the blockchain chain ID

operator_address.description: OperatorAddress is the operator wallet address

phone_calls_enabled.description: PhoneCallsEnabled indicates whether Bland phone calling is available

settlement_recipient.description: SettlementRecipient is the address that receives settlement payments

is_test.description: If true, generates anuma_test_ prefix; otherwise anuma_live_

referral.description: Rewardful referral ID for affiliate tracking
Rewardful referral ID for affiliate tracking

allowed_origins.description: allowed CORS origins for API key requests

claimed_import_rewards.description: Which import providers have been claimed

redirect_uri.description: Optional - uses config default if not provided

cancel_url.description: URL to redirect if payment is cancelled

credits.description: Number of credits to purchase (1 credit = $0.01)

referral.description: Rewardful referral ID for affiliate tracking

success_url.description: URL to redirect after successful payment

status.description: Status indicates the service health status

timestamp.description: Timestamp is the Unix timestamp of the response

version.description: Version is the current API version

is_test.description: If true, generates anuma_test_ prefix; otherwise anuma_live_

default_user_cost_limit_usd.description: Default credits for auto-enrollment in micro-USD

developer_wallet_address.description: Developer wallet (auto-creates account if not exists)

generated_keys.description: Map of app name to generated keys

escrow_contract.description: Required to identify which app enrollment to update

current_period_end.description: Unix timestamp, only present if subscribed

scheduled_interval.description: billing interval of the scheduled plan

scheduled_plan.description: tier user will switch to at period end

refresh_token.description: May not be present on refresh

balance_updated_at.description: When balance was last synced from chain

enrolled_app_id.description: App ID for enrollment (0 if not enrolled)

latency.description: Latency is the request latency in milliseconds

model_requested.description: ModelRequested is the model that was requested

provider.description: Provider is the LLM provider used (e.g., "openai", "anthropic")

image_model.description:
ImageModel is the user-selected image generation model.
When set, the portal overrides the model field in image tool call arguments.

stream.description: Stream indicates if response should be streamed

tools.description: Tools is an array of tool schemas describing which tools the model can use

client_injected_tools.description: ClientInjectedTools are tool names the client provided in the original request.

inference_id.description: InferenceID is the unique identifier for this inference request

messages.description:
Messages contains the full conversation history when local tools need execution.
This is populated when the model requests tools that are not MCP tools (local/client-side tools).
The client should execute these tools and send a new request with this message history
plus the tool results appended.

portal_injected_tools.description: PortalInjectedTools are tool names the portal's classifier added to the request.

tool_call_events.description: ToolCallEvents is an array of tool call events.

tools_checksum.description: ToolsChecksum is the checksum of the tool schemas used by the AI Portal.

completion_tokens.description: CompletionTokens is the number of tokens in the completion

cost_micro_usd.description: CostMicroUSD is the cost of this completion in micro-dollars (USD × 1,000,000)

credits_used.description: CreditsUsed is the number of credits consumed by this completion (ceiling of cost / MicroUSDPerCredit)

prompt_tokens.description: PromptTokens is the number of tokens in the prompt

tool_cost_micro_usd.description: ToolCostMicroUSD is the cost of MCP tool calls in micro-dollars (subset of CostMicroUSD)

total_tokens.description: TotalTokens is the total number of tokens used

finish_reason.description: FinishReason indicates why the completion stopped

chunk_index.description: ChunkIndex is the chunk index (0 for single requests)

latency.description: Latency is the request latency in milliseconds

model_requested.description: ModelRequested is the model that was requested

provider.description: Provider is the LLM provider used (e.g., "openai", "anthropic")

dimensions.description: Dimensions is the number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have (optional)

encoding_format.description: EncodingFormat is the format to return the embeddings in (optional: "float" or "base64")

input.description: Input text or tokens to embed (can be string, []string, []int, or [][]int)

inference_id.description: InferenceID is the unique identifier for this inference request

cost_micro_usd.description: CostMicroUSD is the inference cost for this embedding request

credits_used.description: CreditsUsed is the number of credits consumed by this embedding request

prompt_tokens.description: PromptTokens is the number of tokens in the prompt

total_tokens.description: TotalTokens is the total number of tokens used

description.description: Description is the description of the tool

input_schema.description: InputSchema is the JSON schema describing the tool's input

name.description: Name is the name of the tool

tool_call_id.description: ToolCallID is the ID of the tool call this message is responding to (only for tool role)

tool_calls.description: ToolCalls contains tool/function calls made by the assistant (only for assistant role)

type.description: Type is the message type (for Responses API: "message")

llmapi.MessageContentFile.description: File is used when Type=input_file (for Responses API)

file_data.description: FileData is the base64-encoded file content

file_id.description: FileID is the ID of an uploaded file

file_url.description: FileURL is the URL to the file

filename.description: Filename is the name of the file

llmapi.MessageContentImage.description: ImageURL is used when Type=image_url or Type=input_image

detail.description: Detail is the OpenAI detail hint (auto|low|high)

url.description: URL is the image URL or data URI

text.description: Text holds the text content when Type=text or Type=input_text

type.description: Type is the block type (`text`, `image_url`, or `input_file`)

canonical_slug.description: CanonicalSlug is the canonical slug for the model

context_length.description: ContextLength is the maximum context length in tokens

created.description: Created is the Unix timestamp of when the model was created

description.description: Description describes the model and its capabilities

hugging_face_id.description: HuggingFaceID is the Hugging Face model identifier

max_input_tokens.description: MaxInputTokens is the maximum input tokens

max_output_tokens.description: MaxOutputTokens is the maximum output tokens

modalities.description: Modalities is a list of supported modalities (e.g., ["llm", "vision"])

name.description: Name is the human-readable model name (optional)

owned_by.description: OwnedBy is the organization that owns the model

supported_methods.description: SupportedMethods is a list of supported API methods

supported_parameters.description: SupportedParameters is a list of supported parameter names

llmapi.ModelArchitecture.description: Architecture describes the model's technical capabilities

llmapi.ModelPricing.description: Pricing contains the pricing structure for using this model

llmapi.ModelTopProvider.description: TopProvider contains configuration details for the primary provider

chunk_index.description: ChunkIndex is the chunk index (0 for single requests)

latency.description: Latency is the request latency in milliseconds

data.description: Data contains the list of available models

next_page_token.description: NextPageToken is the token to retrieve the next page of results (omitted if no more pages)

latency.description: Latency is the request latency in milliseconds

model_requested.description: ModelRequested is the model that was requested

provider.description: Provider is the LLM provider used (e.g., "openai", "anthropic")

llmapi.ResponseInput.description:
Input can be a simple text string or an array of messages for multi-turn conversations.
When continuing after client tool calls, pass the messages array from the previous response.

messages.description: Messages is set when input is an array of messages (for multi-turn/tool continuations)

text.description: Text is set when input is a simple string

arguments.description: Arguments is the function arguments for function_call and mcp_call types

call_id.description: CallID is the call ID for function_call and mcp_call types

content.description: Content is the content array for message and reasoning types

error.description: Error is the MCP error message for mcp_call types

id.description: ID is the unique identifier for this output item

name.description: Name is the function name for function_call and mcp_call types

output.description: Output is the MCP tool output for mcp_call types

role.description: Role is the role for message types (e.g., "assistant")

server_label.description: ServerLabel is the MCP server label for mcp_call and mcp_list_tools types

status.description: Status is the status of this output item (e.g., "completed")

summary.description: Summary is the reasoning summary for reasoning types

tools.description: Tools is the list of available tools for mcp_list_tools types

type.description: Type is the output item type (e.g., "message", "function_call", "reasoning", "mcp_call")

llmapi.ResponseReasoning.description: Reasoning configures reasoning for o-series and other reasoning models

background.description: Background indicates if request should be processed in background

image_model.description:
ImageModel is the user-selected image generation model.
When set, the portal overrides the model field in image tool call arguments.

max_output_tokens.description: MaxOutputTokens is the maximum number of tokens to generate

model.description: Model is the model identifier in 'provider/model' format

stream.description: Stream indicates if response should be streamed

tools.description: Tools is an array of tool schemas describing which tools the model can use

client_injected_tools.description: ClientInjectedTools are tool names the client provided in the original request.

created_at.description: Created is the Unix timestamp of creation (created_at in OpenAI format)

id.description: ID is the unique response identifier

messages.description:
Messages contains the full conversation history when local tools need execution.
This is populated when the model requests tools that are not MCP tools (local/client-side tools).
The client should execute these tools and send a new request with this message history
plus the tool results appended.

model.description: Model is the model used for generation

output.description: Output is the array of output items (OpenAI Responses API format)

portal_injected_tools.description: PortalInjectedTools are tool names the portal's classifier added to the request.

tool_call_events.description: ToolCallEvents is an array of tool call events.

tools_checksum.description: ToolsChecksum is the checksum of the tool schemas used by the AI Portal.

completion_tokens.description: CompletionTokens is the number of tokens in the completion

cost_micro_usd.description: CostMicroUSD is the cost of this response in micro-dollars (USD × 1,000,000)

credits_used.description: CreditsUsed is the number of credits consumed by this response

prompt_tokens.description: PromptTokens is the number of tokens in the prompt

tool_cost_micro_usd.description: ToolCostMicroUSD is the cost of MCP tool calls in micro-dollars (subset of CostMicroUSD)

total_tokens.description: TotalTokens is the total number of tokens used

llmapi.Role.description: Role is the message role (system, user, assistant, tool)

llmapi.ThinkingOptions.description: Thinking configures extended thinking for Anthropic models

budget_tokens.description: BudgetTokens is the token budget for thinking

type.description: Type indicates if thinking is enabled: "enabled" or "disabled"

id.description: ID is the unique identifier for this tool call

type.description: Type is the type of tool call (always "function" for now)

llmapi.ToolCallFunction.description: Function contains the function call details

arguments.description: Arguments is the JSON string of arguments to pass to the function

name.description: Name is the name of the function to call

info.description: The Anuma API provides OpenAI-compatible REST and streaming interfaces for building AI applications with access to models from multiple providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and more. You can create chat completions, generate embeddings, and use built-in tools, all with wallet-based authentication and on-chain credit management.
info.title: Anuma Portal API

post.description: Adds credits to a user's account. The settlement worker will handle on-chain enrollment when processing requests.

get.description: Returns all registered apps with pagination. Requires admin API key.

parameters.description: Maximum number of apps to return (default 50, max 100)
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Creates a new app. Requires admin API key.

get.description: Returns API keys for the specified app with pagination. Requires admin API key.

parameters.description: Maximum number of API keys to return (default 50, max 100)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of API keys to skip (default 0)
parameters.name: offset

get.summary: List API keys for an app

post.description: Creates a new API key for an app. Requires admin API key.

delete.description: Deletes an API key by ID. Requires admin API key.

get.description: Returns a single API key by its ID with wallet balance details. Requires admin API key.

put.description: Updates an existing API key. Only provided fields are updated. Requires admin API key.

delete.description: Deletes an app by ID. Requires admin API key.

get.description: Returns a single app by its ID. Requires admin API key.

put.description: Updates an existing app. Only provided fields are updated. Requires admin API key.

post.description: Seeds apps and their API keys into the database. Uses upsert - existing apps are updated. Requires admin API key.
Sets a user's subscription tier (basic, starter, or pro). Requires admin API key.
Generates a chat completion using the configured gateway. Supports streaming when stream=true.

get.description: Returns public configuration including contract addresses and registered apps
Returns the credit balance and related information for the authenticated user. Optionally accepts X-Timezone header for accurate next claim time calculation.

403.description: Balance endpoint not available for this app

post.description: Allows authenticated users to claim free daily credits. Limited to once per calendar day in user's timezone. Requires X-Timezone header with IANA timezone (e.g., "America/New_York").

400.description: Already claimed today or invalid timezone

403.description: Daily credits not available for this app

post.description: Claims a one-time task reward (e.g., import memories from a chat provider). Each task can only be claimed once per account. The request must include the extracted memories as proof of import completion.

400.description: Invalid task type or already claimed

get.description: Returns available credit packs with prices fetched from Stripe. Pro users receive bonus credits on the same pack.

post.description: Creates a Stripe Checkout Session for purchasing a one-time credit pack and returns the checkout URL. Pro users are charged using the Pro product with bonus credits.
Queries Snag for the user's current loyalty points and converts any new points to Portal credits using the configured conversion rate.

get.description: Lists all apps owned by the authenticated developer with pagination.

parameters.description: Maximum number of apps to return (default 50, max 100)
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Creates a new app owned by the authenticated developer.

delete.description: Soft-deletes an app by deactivating it. The app can be reactivated later.

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific app owned by the developer.

patch.description: Updates an app's settings. Only provided fields are updated.

get.description: Lists all API keys for the app (without secrets) with pagination.

parameters.description: Maximum number of API keys to return (default 50, max 100)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of API keys to skip (default 0)
parameters.name: offset

post.description: Creates a new API key for the app. The full key is only shown once.
Creates a Stripe checkout session to purchase credits for the app balance

delete.description: Removes Privy authentication configuration from an app.

post.description: Configures Privy authentication for an app.

get.description: Returns aggregate usage data and timeseries for an app within a time range.

parameters.description: Start time (RFC3339). Defaults to 30 days ago.
parameters.name: start_time

get.description: Returns per-user usage data for an app within a time range, paginated.

parameters.description: Start time (RFC3339). Defaults to 30 days ago.
parameters.name: start_time

get.description: Lists all users enrolled in the app with their credit balances.

parameters.description: Maximum number of users to return (default 50, max 200)
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific user enrolled in the app.

patch.description: Updates a user's cost limit. Credits are transferred from the app balance.

post.description: Adds credits to a user's enrollment. Credits are transferred from the app balance atomically.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of the developer's completed app funding payments.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return (default 50, max 100)
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Returns the OpenAPI 3.1 specification for this API

post.description: Generates embeddings using the configured gateway.

get.description: Returns a list of all available models from the configured gateway with optional filters. Models include modality information indicating their capabilities (e.g., llm, embedding, vision, image, audio, reasoning, code, reranker, multimodal, video).

parameters.description: Number of models to return per page
parameters.name: page_size

parameters.description: Token to get next page of results
parameters.name: page_token

post.description: Queues an AI phone call on behalf of the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns the latest Bland.ai call status, summary, and transcript details for a queued phone call.

post.description: Generates a response using the Responses API format. Supports streaming when stream=true.
Cancels the user's subscription at the end of the current billing period (cancel_at_period_end)
Cancels a scheduled plan downgrade by releasing the Stripe Subscription Schedule, keeping the current plan active.
Creates a Stripe Checkout Session for a subscription plan and returns the checkout URL. Identify the plan with either price_id directly, or tier ("starter"/"pro") + interval ("month"/"year").

requestBody.description: Checkout session request with redirect URLs

post.description: Creates a Stripe Customer Portal session for managing subscription and returns the portal URL

requestBody.description: Customer portal request with return URL

get.description: Returns available subscription plans with prices fetched from Stripe.

post.description: Reactivates a subscription that was scheduled for cancellation (undoes cancel_at_period_end)
Schedules a plan downgrade (tier or interval) to take effect at the end of the current billing period using Stripe Subscription Schedules.

requestBody.description: Downgrade request with target tier and optional interval

get.description: Returns the current subscription status, plan, and billing period info

post.description: Upgrades the current subscription to a higher tier or from monthly to annual billing by modifying the existing Stripe subscription in-place. No extra credits are allocated for the current month; the new plan's credit amount starts at the next billing cycle.

requestBody.description: Upgrade request with target tier and optional interval

post.description: Receives and processes Stripe webhook events for subscription lifecycle management

get.description: Returns all active reward tasks that users can complete
Looks up an active text channel registration by identifier. Requires service-level API key authentication. Results are scoped to the calling app.

parameters.description: Channel identifier (e.g., E.164 phone number for SMS)
parameters.name: identifier

get.summary: Lookup text channel registration by identifier

post.description: Registers an identifier (phone, email, etc.) for text channel interaction. The identifier must be linked in the user's Privy account (for SMS). Idempotent for the same account+channel (updates preferred model).

400.description: Invalid channel, identifier, or identifier not in linked accounts

get.description: Returns the text channel registration status for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Deactivates the text channel registration for the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns a map of available MCP tool schemas indexed by tool name.
Returns per-model usage (spend, requests, tokens) and tool usage grouped by model for the authenticated user within a time period.

parameters.description: Start of date range in RFC 3339 format (e.g. 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Must be used with end. Takes precedence over period.
parameters.name: start

parameters.description: End of date range in RFC 3339 format (e.g. 2024-01-31T23:59:59Z). Must be used with start. Takes precedence over period.
parameters.name: end

parameters.description: Time period. Day aliases: 7d, 30d, 90d, 180d, 365d. Durations: 10m, 30m, 1h, 6h, 12h, 24h, 72h. Default: 30d. Max: 365d.
parameters.name: period

post.description: Exchanges an OAuth authorization code for access and refresh tokens
Refreshes an expired access token using a refresh token
Revokes an OAuth access or refresh token

get.description: Returns the current health status of the service.
